(SEE MINI MINUTES AND WOD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS) PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 30 DAYS (FEBRUARY 7, 2022) LEAVE TO AMEND AND FILE AN APPROPRIATE MOTION SEEKING AN ORDER ALLOWING JANE DOE DESIGNATION. MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
MICHAEL R. DAYMUDE, ESQ. 818.971.9409, michael@mrdaymude.com, for Plaintiff, JANE DOE.
JOAN E. TRIMBLE, Esq., CALLAHAN, THOMPSON, SHERMAN& CAUDILL, LLP, (949) 261-2872, jtrimble@ctsclaw.com, for Defendant, SHAWN McCALL and BAY PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSOCIATES.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SHAWN MCCALL and BAY PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT Off calendar. The demurrer ruling resolves this motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
MICHAEL R. DAYMUDE, ESQ. 818.971.9409, michael@mrdaymude.com, for Plaintiff, JANE DOE.
JOAN E. TRIMBLE, Esq., CALLAHAN, THOMPSON, SHERMAN& CAUDILL, LLP, (949) 261-2872, jtrimble@ctsclaw.com, for Defendant, SHAWN McCALL and BAY PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSOCIATES.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

DEFENDANT SHAWN MCCALL, BAY PSYCHOLOGICAL ASSOCIATES, INC. DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT

(SEE WORD MINTUES FOR COMPLETE DETAILS)

Demurrer to Plaintiff Jane Doe's Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action and sustained with leave to amend as to the second and fourth cause of action.

The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for Sexual Harassment in Defined Relationship under Section 51.9 is sustained with leave to amend.

The demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with leave to amend.

The demurrer to the fifth cause of action, Retaliation in Violation of Gov. Code section 12940(h)) is sustained without leave to amend.

Finally, the Court denies the motion to strike the punitive damages per CCP section 425.13 based on the Court's ruling sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend as set forth above. Furthermore, the Court orders Plaintiff to file a motion seeking an order allowing her to proceed under the Jane Doe designation. (CCP sec. 367; Doe v. Lincoln Unified School Dist. (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 758, 767.)

Plaintiff shall have 30 days (February 7, 2022) leave to amend and file an appropriate motion seeking an order allowing Jane Doe designation.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
